title: Depth of Shinji and Asukas relationship
display_title: Depth of Shinji and Asukas relationship
wikitext:
[]: Depth of Shinji and Asuka's relationship             Quote Post by Ocelot » Tue Aug 26, 2008 1:58 pm Of all the relationships in the series this one is the most complex. I think it's obvious it's sexual and extremely evident, but what is the extent to which Shinji and Asuka acknowledge it? I found the end of EoE to be that they both acknowledged it and could finally be happy, after both of them trying to disconnect themselves (comatose scene, scene in the apartment)

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Tue Aug 26, 2008 2:02 pm http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php ... sc&start=0 I think they both love each other, but they don't have enough common sense to put theirselves out there. (Why's it called common sense when 90% of people don't have it?)

[]:             Quote Post by Ocelot » Tue Aug 26, 2008 2:11 pm If you listen to Komm susser Tod, the lyrics seem like it's Shinji thinking about Asuka and vice versa, them finally realizing their love.

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Tue Aug 26, 2008 2:15 pm Therefor, ReTake is the perfect follow-up for EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by Ocelot » Tue Aug 26, 2008 2:25 pm Re-Take is actually really good and if fleshed out more (no pun intended) it would be good canon material. The things I liked about EoE and Re-Take were how graphic they were. Not because I only like sex and violence, but the series is extremely graphic within the characters own heads yet it isn't reflected as well on the outside.

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Tue Aug 26, 2008 2:28 pm Exactly! And I like that you have to actually pay attention and it's not something you just watch and you know what's going on.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Aug 26, 2008 2:31 pm

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Aug 26, 2008 2:50 pm My god, they have the be the most dysfunctional relationship I've EVER seen. I'm so glad that the "Adam and Eve" EoE theory isn't true or humanity is F*CKED!

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Tue Aug 26, 2008 2:54 pm Have to agree with you there.

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Tue Aug 26, 2008 2:57 pm Why was Shinji strangling Asuka at the end? If he wanted to do it wouldn't it have happened earlier?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Aug 26, 2008 3:11 pm LeoXiao wrote: Why was Shinji strangling Asuka at the end? If he wanted to do it wouldn't it have happened earlier?

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Tue Aug 26, 2008 3:31 pm Wow that took me a while to work my way through. The [inter]Net is vast and infinite.

[]:             Quote Post by SeanTucker » Tue Aug 26, 2008 3:46 pm AuraTwilight wrote: My god, they have the be the most dysfunctional relationship I've EVER seen. I'm so glad that the "Adam and Eve" EoE theory isn't true or humanity is F*CKED!

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Tue Aug 26, 2008 3:47 pm Ahh, the imagination of some people...

[]:             Quote Post by swamp147 » Tue Aug 26, 2008 3:49 pm SeanTucker wrote: which is to say she's somehow pregnant, presumably with Shinji's kid.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Aug 26, 2008 3:57 pm SeanTucker wrote: one interpretation of Asuka's last line in the subbed version is that she feels sick

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Tue Aug 26, 2008 4:17 pm Shinji and Asuka are fucked-up little kiddies who shouldn't be paired with anyone until they've gotten some counseling.

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Tue Aug 26, 2008 4:19 pm That statement can also be directed at millions of people in the world today. Whose to say we all don't have problems that diserve counseling.

[]: Re: Depth of Shinji and Asuka's relationship             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Aug 26, 2008 4:43 pm Trying to get back to TOPIC, BITCHES Ocelot wrote: Of all the relationships in the series this one is the most complex. I think it's obvious it's sexual and extremely evident, but what is the extent to which Shinji and Asuka acknowledge it?

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Tue Aug 26, 2008 6:52 pm IrkenEvangelion wrote: I think they both love each other, but they don't have enough common sense to put theirselves out there. (Why's it called common sense when 90% of people don't have it?)
